quotes.verbatim_quote:
To begin exploring eighth-note rhythms start by playing 4 consecutive eighth notes as a one-bar

rhythm. Next, displace the grouping by starting on eight different place#in the bar.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core pedagogical instruction defining the displacement method all eight rhythmic groupings build upon.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
For #6, #7, and #8, the harmony can be anticipated. That is, starting on the “and” of three, beat four
or the “and” of four, you can anticipate the next chord by either approaching notes of the next
chord or by simply sounding the next chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines the rule for harmonic anticipation on late-beat rhythmic entries.

quotes.verbatim_quote: 5. On this rhythm the last eighth note would sound the next chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Specific rule about how a rhythmic displacement creates automatic harmonic anticipation.

quotes.topic: Eighth notes vs. quarter notes notation
quotes.verbatim_quote:
Note that many of the eighth-note downbeats might have been written as guarter notes. In these
preceding pages they were written as eighth notes to keep clear the connection to the original 22
thythms.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explains a deliberate notational choice that clarifies the method's logic.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Having played through these various displacements of each rhythm you can now combine two of
the one-bar rhythms. Create a two-bar rhythm and play it through a tune. Combine three one-bar
rhythms and create a three-bar rhythm and play it through a tune. Combine four one-bar rhythms
and create a four-bar rhythm and play it through’a tune.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Progression instruction from isolated one-bar patterns to multi-bar phrases.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Next, try the following four-note grouping which will result in the superimposition of one time
signature over another. This is a 3/4 pattern which takes three bars of 4/4 to work out.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces metric superimposition extending eighth-note displacement into polyrhythm.

quotes.verbatim_quote: 9. Try playing four consecutive eighth notes on different beats randomly, for example:
quotes.why_load_bearing: Shifts student from prescribed patterns to free exploration.
